_doc: Advisories listed here will NOT fail the dependency-audit job until their `expires` date.

STRICT POLICY: only entries with `severity` of `low` or `moderate` are accepted. High and critical advisories are never waivable and must be fixed at source (upgrade, override, or drop the dependency). Entries declaring high/critical severity are rejected by apply-allowlist.mjs at validation time, and a high/critical advisory at runtime always fails the gate even if a matching id is present.
After `expires` (UTC, YYYY-MM-DD), the entry is treated as inactive and the gate fails again.
An entry matches when its `id` equals any of the advisory's ruleId, GHSA id, or one of its CVE ids.
All fields except `id`, `severity`, and `expires` are documentation only.
Audit JSON parsing and matching live in .github/workflows/scripts/apply-allowlist.mjs.

entries.reason: Pinned to react-router 6.30.4 per mem://architecture/dependency-pinning; upstream fix only in 7.x. Mitigation: no <Link>/useNavigate/Navigate call site accepts a user-controlled `to` value (verified: `rg -n "to=\{" src/ | rg -v "'/|\"/"` returns only static literals). Reviewer check: re-run that ripgrep on the PR diff; if it introduces a dynamic `to`, block the waiver.

Pinned to react-router 6.30.4 per mem://architecture/dependency-pinning; upstream fix only in 7.x. Mitigation: no route accepts a user-controlled path starting with `//` (verified: all `navigate(...)` targets in `src/` are string literals or values from our own routing constants, never `location.search`/`hash`/query params). Reviewer check: `rg -n "navigate\(" src/` — every match must resolve to an internal literal path.

Open-redirect via backslash in <Link>/useNavigate (CVE-2025-68470 bypass); fixed only in react-router 7.18.0+. Pinned to v6 per mem://architecture/dependency-pinning. Mitigation: (a) no `<Link to>` or `navigate(...)` argument is derived from `window.location`, query strings, or request bodies; (b) social-OAuth `redirect_uri` is a same-origin literal built from `window.location.origin` per mem://auth/social-sign-on. Reviewer check: `rg -n "to=\{|navigate\(" src/` — flag any dynamic value from user input.

Arbitrary Constructor Injection via deserializeErrors() in React Router SSR hydration; fixed only in react-router 7.18.0+. Pinned to v6 per mem://architecture/dependency-pinning. Mitigation: this project is a Vite SPA (`vite build` → static assets, no SSR/StaticHandler/createStaticRouter usage) so the vulnerable hydration path is unreachable at runtime. Reviewer check: `rg -n "StaticRouter|createStaticRouter|StaticHandler|renderToString" src/` must return no results.

react-router-dom re-export of the same open-redirect-to-XSS advisory that affects react-router v6 (CVE-2025-68470 family); fix only ships in react-router-dom 7.18.0+. Pinned to v6 per mem://architecture/dependency-pinning. Mitigation is identical to the react-router entries above: no `<Link to>` or `navigate(...)` argument is derived from user-controlled input (`window.location`, query strings, request bodies), and OAuth `redirect_uri` is a same-origin literal built from `window.location.origin` per mem://auth/social-sign-on. Reviewer check: `rg -n "to=\{|navigate\(" src/` — flag any dynamic value from user input.
Advisory affects postcss < 8.4.31. Project installs postcss 8.5.12+ (override), so the runtime is not vulnerable. Allowlisted to silence a stale advisory match from the audit database.
